LDO recommend using tantalum over ceramic, because the regulation also needs some damper (milli-volt)

Label: CONTROL_1
Alt Text: A physical signal propogation diagram showing how data comes into the control board, moves through it, then leaves. It has 1: differential pare voltage from preamp goes to 2: PreAmp+ and - goes to an opAmp that is a BufferAmp that acts as a low pass filter. This goes to 3: ADC then 4: off ot the microconroller over I2C. Not shown the but microcontrol reads and updates the state (updates the piezo as needed, or other voltages such as bias during initiailization). 5. Data from microcontroller returns and goes to a DAC which gets Piezo state and has 4 channels of data. 6 an opAmp circuit done in four parts is signaled which takes care of adding and subtracting Z+X, Z-X, Z+Y, and Z-Y. Lastly 7: this is output on a port to the piezo as it's control voltages to control it's movement/flex. This is a critical diagram

Label: SOFTWARE_5
Alt Text: A view of the voltage on the peizo Z during the approach sequence. Notice how the piezo protrudes as the saw wave decends in steps, then pauses briefly as the stepper motor takes another larger scale approach step. The stepper motor step is consider coarse, add only the peize Z-axis probe steps are considered fine approach. All forward approach is stopped as soon as quantum tunneling current above a threshold defined as 500 in the GUI is detected.
